methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Tobe Energy is a seed-stage climate and energy company founded by Colby DeWeese and Caleb Lareau that has raised $4.6M to date. The Climate & Energy sector includes 9 peer companies across public and private markets—1 public (LNZA) and 8 private entities including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen—though comparables at the same stage and valuation tier are limited.

Stage-progression analysis, the only methodologically sound approach given available data, applies a 2.5x multiplier to the $4.6M raised as a valuation proxy, yielding $11.5M. This reflects typical expansion-stage step-ups observed in seed-to-next-stage transitions within the sector. Peer-median comparison was not applicable; while 9 same-sector peers exist, insufficient overlap in stage and valuation tier (fewer than 3 datapoints meeting narrow filters) would have introduced substantial error.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $10M to $13M, with a median implied value of $12M. Key uncertainties center on capital efficiency relative to frontier-tech benchmarks, near-term runway extension, and the absence of tighter stage-matched comparables in this nascent sector. This range reflects seed-stage positioning but carries meaningful sensitivity to team execution and capital deployment trajectory over the next 12–18 months.
